title: If you aren't terrified by this heatwave, you should be
description: The extreme heat currently being felt in Europe isn’t the new normal – much worse is to come, and we are doing far too little to adapt, says Michael Le Page
author: Michael Le Page

title: Record-breaking IBM chip uses trick to cram in 100 billion transistors
description: IBM's latest chip packs in twice as many transistors as the current state-of-the-art chip by adding a second layer of silicon circuitry
author: Matthew Sparkes

title: Bacteria-killing viruses redirect vaccine immunity to destroy cancer
description: Phages, viruses that infect bacteria, could be genetically manipulated to destroy cancerous cells using the immunity we have acquired from vaccines
author: Tom Bawden

title: Lost books by ancient philosophers recovered from 'unreadable' scrolls
description: Scrolls from the Roman library of Herculaneum that were carbonised by a volcanic eruption have been read in their entirety for the first time, thanks to scans and AI software
author: Hayley Bennett

title: Possible signs of ancient life on Mars are rich in complex carbon
description: An instrument on the Perseverance rover has identified large, complex carbon compounds alongside unusual patterns on the surface of rocks that resemble traces of microbial activity
author: Alex Wilkins

title: A note from the anniversary editor
description: Send us your memories of New Scientist for our 70th anniversary issue
author: Sam Wong
